key_insight: svMultiPhysics is an open-source finite element solver that unifies cardiovascular solid mechanics, fluid flow, diffusion, and electrophysiology in a modular multiphysics framework.
engineering_relevance: It enables patient-specific cardiovascular simulation and helps researchers study tightly coupled heart and vessel processes needed for diagnosis, treatment planning, and mechanistic analysis.
method: C++ parallel finite element solver for PDE-based cardiovascular physics
Supports independent or coupled solution of mechanics, fluids, diffusion, and electrophysiology
Integrates with SimVascular as the 3D solver for image-based modeling
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary GPU-enabled runs achieving up to about 30× wall-clock speedup for selected linear solver configurations over CPU-based simulations.
watch_item: The reported speedup is preliminary and limited to selected linear solver setups, so performance may vary with model size, coupling complexity, and hardware.
paper_title: svMultiPhysics: a finite element-based solver for cardiovascular simulations
